skill.name: seo-optimizer
skill.description: Audit WordPress SEO settings for Yoast SEO and Rank Math. Check focus keywords, meta descriptions, featured images, and image metadata.
skill.summary: Find missing WordPress SEO fields and produce practical page-level recommendations.
skill.author: CrazySwami

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak-cryptography matches, but those are false positives caused by SEO terms and report labels. Confirmed concerns are external command execution, WordPress database access, network requests, and a hidden Claude MCP config reference; no malicious intent or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution for Docker and Curl
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: audit.py invokes docker exec mysql and curl through subprocess.run. The calls use argument arrays and do not use shell=True, so command injection evidence is limited, but running local containers and fetching URLs is sensitive behavior for a marketplace skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess.run calls are direct and confirmed. Confidence is not higher because arguments are not passed through a shell and the behavior appears related to the stated SEO audit purpose.

medium_findings.title: Direct WordPress Database Access with Hardcoded Local Credentials
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: audit.py stores local WordPress database connection values and runs SQL through docker exec mysql. The script performs SELECT queries, while SKILL.md also documents direct SQL update patterns, which can bypass WordPress permission checks if followed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded database settings and direct SQL execution are explicit. The highest impact update behavior is documented in SKILL.md rather than implemented in audit.py.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Claude MCP Configuration Path Referenced
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md tells users the Unsplash MCP server is configured at /root/.claude/.mcp.json. Hidden agent configuration files can contain connector details or credentials, so marketplace users should review this requirement before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden configuration path is explicitly referenced. I did not find code that reads or exfiltrates the file, so the concern is configuration exposure and operational sensitivity.

low_findings.title: Network Access Is Required for WordPress and SEO Resources
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: audit.py fetches WordPress REST API data with curl, and SKILL.md references external documentation and Unsplash usage. No evidence found of credential exfiltration or covert network destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network behavior is clear from curl and external URLs. The risk is limited because the endpoints are user-supplied WordPress URLs or documentation links.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the many weak cryptography matches appear to be triggered by SEO words such as meta description, markdown examples, output status labels, and display symbols. No hashing, encryption, MD5, SHA-1, or other cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas are SEO description checks and skill documentation. I found no cryptographic API usage in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Command Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: the SKILL.md backtick findings are markdown code fences for meta keys, REST endpoints, example SQL, JSON-shaped reports, and sample output. They are not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are visibly fenced documentation examples. They are not executable code paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Executes Local Programs
dangerous_patterns.description: audit.py runs docker and curl as subprocesses. This is legitimate for the stated audit workflow but should be treated as sensitive because it depends on local system state and network input.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The calls are explicit subprocess.run invocations. No shell=True usage or obfuscated command construction was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct Database Query Construction
dangerous_patterns.description: audit.py builds SQL strings for WordPress metadata lookup and passes them to mysql. Values are expected to be WordPress post IDs, but direct SQL should still be reviewed before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The SQL construction is present and tied to metadata reads. I did not find evidence that arbitrary user input is intentionally accepted for these IDs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Agent Configuration Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md references /root/.claude/.mcp.json as an Unsplash MCP configuration source. Users should avoid exposing or modifying hidden agent configuration without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path is present in the instructions. No code path reads the file in the reviewed files.

content.user_title: Audit WordPress SEO with Yoast and Rank Math
content.value_statement: WordPress teams often miss focus keywords, meta descriptions, and featured image metadata across many pages. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code inspect those fields and produce clear SEO fixes.
actual_capabilities: Audits WordPress pages through REST API responses and local database metadata.
Checks Yoast SEO focus keywords, SEO titles, and meta descriptions.
Checks featured image IDs, titles, file paths, and ALT text.
Scores each audited page and lists page-level SEO issues.
Prints human-readable audit reports or JSON output from audit.py.
Documents optional workflows for finding replacement images through Unsplash.
limitations: The script assumes a local Docker database container with fixed WordPress credentials.
Rank Math support is described, but audit.py reads Yoast-specific meta keys.
Automatic image upload and WordPress content updates are documented but not implemented in audit.py.
The audit cannot judge search intent, conversion quality, or keyword competition.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Small Business Site for Launch
use_cases.description: Check core WordPress pages for missing focus keywords, meta descriptions, and featured images before publication.

use_cases.title: Review Content Operations at Scale
use_cases.description: Run a repeatable audit across pages and custom posts to find metadata gaps for editors.

use_cases.title: Triage SEO Debt During Maintenance
use_cases.description: Identify pages with weak Yoast metadata and produce a prioritized fix list for a maintenance sprint.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Page Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner audit for a WordPress site
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the seo-optimizer skill to audit all WordPress pages at my staging URL. Summarize missing focus keywords, meta descriptions, and featured images.

prompt_templates.title: Check One Important Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review for a high-value page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the seo-optimizer skill to audit only the page slug I provide. Explain the SEO score and list the safest fixes first.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Pages and Posts
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the seo-optimizer skill to audit pages and the property post type. Group the results by issue type and priority.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Editorial Fix Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the seo-optimizer skill output to create a fix plan for editors. Include recommended focus keywords, meta description targets, and image metadata changes.

output_examples.input: Audit all pages on a staging WordPress site.
output_examples.output: A page-by-page SEO report with scores, missing fields, metadata length checks, featured image status, and recommended fixes.
A focused summary showing the page score, focus keyword status, meta description quality, image ALT text, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Prepare a content team fix list.
output_examples.output: A prioritized editorial checklist grouped by missing keywords, missing descriptions, weak image metadata, and pages needing image selection.

best_practices: Run audits against staging or read-only environments before changing production content.
Review generated keyword and meta description suggestions before publishing them.
Confirm Docker database credentials and MCP configuration before enabling Bash or network actions.
anti_patterns: Do not run direct database commands on production without a backup and approval.
Do not treat keyword presence as the only measure of SEO quality.
Do not use Unsplash images without checking license, relevance, and brand fit.

faq.question: Does this skill support Yoast SEO?
faq.answer: Yes. audit.py reads Yoast focus keyword, meta description, SEO title, and featured image metadata.

faq.question: Does this skill fully support Rank Math?
faq.answer: Rank Math is described in the documentation, but the included script reads Yoast-specific metadata.

faq.question: Can it update WordPress content automatically?
faq.answer: The documentation describes update workflows, but audit.py only audits and reports findings.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: Yes. The script fetches WordPress REST API data, and the workflow may use Unsplash or SEO documentation.

faq.question: Is it safe for production sites?
faq.answer: Use caution. The script runs local commands and reads database metadata, so staging review is recommended first.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
